creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
> boot_verbose=1 in /boot/loader.conf and reboot

boot_verbose set higher verbose only for hardware, not for logical issues

but I set kern.geom.label.debug=1 and got this:

GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (ada0s2, EBR)
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s2: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s3: no FAT signature found.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s4: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on ada0s4.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada0s4 is ext2fs/Arch.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada1: no FAT signature found.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada1 is diskid/DISK-WD-WCC6Y2XD2TAE.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada2: FAT12/16 volume not valid.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada2 is diskid/DISK-WD-WMAP9C839791.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s1: no FAT signature found.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s1.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label LinuxMint(ext2fs/LinuxMint) already exists (diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s1).
GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s2, EBR)
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s2: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s3: no FAT signature found.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s4: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s4.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label Arch(ext2fs/Arch) already exists (diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916s4).
.... 
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label gptid/c4cc5394-68e8-11ee-930b-9f6cf58cec0b removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label diskid/DISK-WD-WCAYUEP01916 removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label diskid/DISK-WD-WCC6Y2XD2TAE removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label ext2fs/LinuxMint removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s1: no FAT signature found.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on ada0s1.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada0s1 is ext2fs/LinuxMint.
ext2fs: ada0s2: wrong magic number 0 (expected 0xef53)
GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (ada0s2, EBR)
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s2: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label ext2fs/Arch removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s4: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on ada0s4.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada0s4 is ext2fs/Arch.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label ext2fs/Arch removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: MSDOSFS: ada0s4: no FAT volume detected.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: ext2fs file system detected on ada0s4.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label for provider ada0s4 is ext2fs/Arch.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label ext2fs/Arch removed.
GEOM_LABEL[1]: Label ext2fs/LinuxMint removed.

at this moment I don't fully understand the meaning, I have to read it up

then here another funny part, for now I adapted fstab, now trying to mount fstab entries

root@hm-fbsd:/home/hm # mount /Arch
fstab: /etc/fstab:13: Inappropriate file type or format
fstab: /etc/fstab:14: Inappropriate file type or format
mount: /Arch: unknown special file or file system

root@hm-fbsd:/home/hm # mount /Linux_Mint/
fstab: /etc/fstab:13: Inappropriate file type or format
fstab: /etc/fstab:14: Inappropriate file type or format
mount: /Linux_Mint: unknown special file or file system

fstab:
/dev/ada0p1             /Linux_Mint     ext2fs  noauto  2       0
/dev/ada0p4             /Arch           ext2fs  noauto  2       0
#/dev/ada0p5             /Arch          ext2fs  noauto  2       0

it doen't matter if I set s1 or p1 in fstab, the outcome is the same

BUT!

root@hm-fbsd:/home/hm # mount -t ext2fs /dev/ada0s4 /Arch
root@hm-fbsd:/home/hm # mount -t ext2fs /dev/ada0s1 /Linux_Mint
root@hm-fbsd:/home/hm # df -h
relevant lines ...
/dev/ada0s4     18G     15G    2.3G    86%    /Arch
/dev/ada0s1     58G     35G     23G    60%    /Linux_Mint

and at the same time root is mounted as
/dev/ada2p3     48G     18G     27G    40%    /


I can understand better now that the main problem is with ada0 partitioning, I don't know how to fix that without another disk and a data backup

nevertheless, on Freebsd it doesn't explain the mismatch between s and p, but then mounting with full cmd but not from fstab whith identical configs for the same partitions or slides, as well as the not consisting error messages 

even if I'm ignoring the confusing error messages, it should mount the fstab entry without complaints, when it mounts the same configs from a cmd line
